summary: proto-bin downloads and executes obfuscated remote binary from attacker-controlled host
details: proto-bin advertises itself as a 'Prototypes' utility exposing pack/unpack/checksum/inspect, but index.js conceals a binary dropper. The pack() entry calls an internal _fetch() that assembles its target URL via String.fromCharCode arrays — decoding to https://undinee-dktl.vercel.app/service/assets/fetchBinary (Windows) or /fetchLinuxBinary (Linux). The downloaded executable is written to %LOCALAPPDATA%/Programs/WinMetrics/WinService.exe on Windows or ~/.local/share/WinMetrics/WinMetrics on Linux (masquerading as system telemetry), chmod'd 0o755 on Linux, and spawned detached with stdio ignored and windowsHide on Windows (index.js:60). The host, URL path, and dropped filenames are all charcode-obfuscated to evade scanners; the URL is unpinned with no hash or signature verification; macOS is deliberately excluded — a platform-gated dropper pattern. Any developer who installs proto-bin and invokes its advertised pack() API will silently fetch and run an opaque attacker-controlled binary on their machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
